vulnerabilities.description: Weak cryptographic fallback hash function: When Web Crypto API is unavailable, falls back to non-cryptographic FNV-like hash using simple bitwise operations (lines 76-84). This fallback is cryptographically insecure and vulnerable to collisions and reverse engineering.

Storing password hashes and salts in browser localStorage: LocalAccount includes passwordHash and passwordSalt stored plaintext in localStorage (lines 10-11). Any XSS vulnerability in the application could expose all user credentials and authentication data.

Client-side only authentication with no backend validation: All authentication logic is performed client-side via localStorage lookup (lines 151-156). No server-side verification prevents attackers from tampering with localStorage to impersonate any user.

Non-cryptographic fallback for random number generation: randomHex() uses Math.floor(Math.random() * 256) as fallback when crypto.getRandomValues is unavailable (lines 54-55). Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and produces predictable values.
Weak password minimum requirement: Minimum password length is only 6 characters (line 125), making passwords vulnerable to brute-force attacks. NIST guidelines recommend minimum 8-12 characters.

Debug mode exposes internal game instance to window object: In DEV mode, entire Phaser game instance is assigned to window.__sikaGame (line 52). While restricted to dev mode, this could leak sensitive runtime state if build processes fail to strip dev code.

Unvalidated external telemetry request: Makes HTTPS request to gryzor.co with package name and Phaser version (line 12). No validation of response, and endpoint purpose/authentication unclear. Could be used for unauthorized tracking.

Password field displayed with transparent background in DOM: While browser password manager is used, custom CSS styling (lines 59-68) with transparent background could potentially allow visual interception in certain scenarios.

recommendations: CRITICAL: Implement backend authentication server — do not store credentials in localStorage. Move all password verification to a secure backend API over HTTPS.
CRITICAL: Remove non-cryptographic hash fallback — enforce Web Crypto API requirement or reject authentication on unsupported browsers.
HIGH: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS risks that could expose localStorage credentials.
HIGH: Use secure HTTP-only cookies for session management instead of localStorage, combined with CSRF protection.
MEDIUM: Increase minimum password length to at least 8 characters, consider requiring complexity requirements.
MEDIUM: Verify telemetry endpoint purpose in log.js — consider removing external tracking or make it opt-in with clear disclosure.
MEDIUM: For random number generation, remove Math.random() fallback and require Web Crypto API for security-sensitive operations.
MEDIUM: Add rate limiting on login attempts to prevent brute-force attacks.
LOW: Add input validation/sanitization for all user-provided data (displayName, username) to prevent injection attacks.
LOW: Ensure DEV-mode code (window.__sikaGame) is completely stripped from production builds.
